sections.text: 5 Section 7.4 Labelling of non-retail containers Information specified in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container, and in the absence of such a container, on the product itself. However, lot identification and the name and address may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to Coulommiers intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to Coulommiers intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to Coulommiers intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Coulommiers is a soft, surface ripened, primarily mould ripened cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) which has a shape of a flat cylinder or sectors thereof. The body has a near white through to light yellow colour and a soft-textured (when pressed by thumb), but not crumbly texture, ripened from the surface to the center of the cheese. Gas holes are generally absent, but few openings and splits are acceptable. A rind is to be developed that is soft and entirely covered with white mould but may have red, brownish or orange coloured spots. Whole cheese may be cut or formed into sectors prior to or after the mould development. For Coulommiers ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 10 days at 10 –16 °C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Coulommiers intended for further processing need not exhibit the same extent of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

Gas holes are generally absent, but few openings and splits are acceptable. A rind is to be developed that is soft and entirely covered with white mould but may have red, brownish or orange coloured spots. Whole cheese may be cut or formed into sectors prior to or after the mould development. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Coulommiers intended for further processing need not exhibit the same extent of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

The body has a near white through to light yellow colour and a soft-textured (when pressed by thumb), but not crumbly texture, ripened from the surface to the center of the cheese. Gas holes are generally absent, but few openings and splits are acceptable. A rind is to be developed that is soft and entirely covered with white mould but may have red, brownish or orange coloured spots. Whole cheese may be cut or formed into sectors prior to or after the mould development. For Coulommiers ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 10 days at 10 –16 °C depending on the extent of maturity required. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3

sections.title: Raw materials
sections.text: Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Raw materials
number=3.1
level=2
Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks.

page_spans.text: Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks.

sections.title: Permitted ingredients
sections.text: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms, including Geotrichum candidum, Brevibacterium linens, and yeast; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti -caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

sections.title: Essential ripening procedure
sections.text: Rind formation and maturation (proteolysis) from the surface to the center is predominantly caused by Penicillium candidum and/or Penicillium camembertii and Penicillium caseicolum.

sections.text: Only those additives classes indicated as justified in the table below may be used for the product categories specified. Colours used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 01.6.2.1 (Ripened cheese, includes rind) and only certain acidity regulators in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name Coulommiers may be applied in accordance with Section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), provided that the product is in conformity with this standard. Where customary in the country of retail sale, alternative spelling may be used. The use of the name is an option that may be chosen only if the cheese complies with this standard. Where the name is not used for a cheese that complies with this standard, the naming provisions of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) apply. The designation of products in which the fat content is above the reference range specified in Section 3.3 of this standard shall be accompanied by an appropriate qualification describing the modification made or the fat content (expressed as fat in dry matter or as percentage by mass whichever is acceptable in the country of retail sale), either as part of the name or in a prominent position in the same field of vision. Suitable qualifiers are the appropriate characterizing terms specified in Section 7.3 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) or a nutritional claim in accordance with the Guidelines for Use of Nutrition and Health Claims (CXG 23-1997)1. The designation may also be used for cut, sliced, shredded or grated products made from cheese which cheese is in conformity with this standard.

sections.title: Country of origin
sections.text: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared. When the product undergoes substantial transformation 2 in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.

sections.title: Declaration of milkfat content
sections.text: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or ( iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

sections.title: Type of coagulation
sections.text: Coagulation of the milk protein is typically obtained through the combined action of microbial acidification and proteases (e.g. rennet) at an appropriate coagulation temperature.

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms, including Geotrichum candidum, Brevibacterium linens, and yeast; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti -caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

prefilter.reason: Contains detailed ingredient specifications; could be regulatory or technical.

classification.sentence: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms, including Geotrichum candidum, Brevibacterium linens, and yeast; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti -caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.
classification.function: Listing permitted substances with conditions for use
classification.pattern: List of [Substances]: Notwithstanding [Provision], these substances can be used as [Function] for [Products] only, provided [Conditions].

components.Substances: Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms, including Geotrichum candidum, Brevibacterium linens, and yeast; Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; Potable water; Safe and suitable processing aids; Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; Rice, corn and potato flours and starches
components.Provision: the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978)
components.Conditions: added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by good manufacturing practice, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4

classification.justification: Complex permissions with exceptions and conditions expressed; no close template match found.

sentences.original: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

classification.sentence: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
classification.function: Mandatory compliance requirement
classification.pattern: Subject (products covered by this Standard) + modal verb (shall) + main verb (comply) + object (Maximum Levels for contaminants specified in another standard).

components.subject: The products covered by this Standard
components.object: the Maximum Levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation (shall comply) for products to meet specified contaminant limits defined elsewhere. While the modality and communicative function are clear, no exact template matches exist. The structure is a typical Compliance Obligation referencing an external standard but is represented here as a new pattern.
notes: Sentence references external standards and compliance but does not fit a known template in the catalog.
Contains an embedded clause describing contaminant levels as specified externally.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.function: Prescriptive Guidance
classification.pattern: Complex compound sentence with embedded normative recommendations and references to other standards

components.subject: product covered by the provisions of this standard
actions: be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of related Codex texts
comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with CXG 21-1997

classification.justification: Sentence prescribes recommended preparation, handling, and compliance expressed as obligations with conditional references, complex multi-part structure not matched to known templates.
notes: Use of recommendation modality but expressed with normative 'should comply' indicates obligation function.

sentences.original: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

prefilter.reason: States applicable standards, relevant for regulation.

classification.sentence: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:
classification.function: Introduce additional applicable provisions conditionally
classification.pattern: Conditional statement introducing further provisions

References: General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)
General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999)

classification.justification: This sentence introduces scope extensions conditionally, a pattern not represented in existing templates catalog.
notes: Signals conditional applicability of further rules.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.function: Prescriptive requirement
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

sentences.original: The name Coulommiers may be applied in accordance with Section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), provided that the product is in conformity with this standard.

sentences.original: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or ( iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

classification.sentence: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or ( iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.
classification.function: Declaration requirement with options and condition
classification.pattern: Compound sentence with mandatory action, enumeration of options, and conditional clause

components.obligation: The milk fat content shall be declared
manner_options: as a percentage of fat in dry matter
in grams per serving as quantified in the label
components.condition: provided that the number of servings is stated

classification.justification: Obligation to declare content with enumerated acceptable options and conditional statement is partially matching common labelling declaration patterns.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR COULOMMIERS
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto estruturado para hierarquia.

Página mapeada com dois títulos principais em caixa alta. O primeiro é '2022 AMENDMENT' com texto explicativo. O segundo título parece ser um cabeçalho intermediário de tabela com texto detalhado. Nenhuma continuidade de seção detectada entre páginas.

Esta página contém três títulos principais numerados: 1. SCOPE, 2. DESCRIPTION e 3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS. Este último é um agrupador de subseções. As subseções 3.1 Raw materials e 3.2 Permitted ingredients estão mapeadas com seus textos correspondentes. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado como 'CXS 274-1969  3'. O texto foi associado corretamente aos seus respectivos títulos para uso em RAG.

A página apresenta cinco seções principais numeradas de nível 1 (3.3 a 5) com seus respectivos textos detalhados. Não há subtítulos nem indicação de continuidade para outras páginas. O cabeçalho da página está identificado como 'CXS 274-1969  4'.

A página 5 apresenta três seções principais numeradas de nível 1 (6, 7, 8) com textos explicativos correspondentes. A seção 7 usa subtítulos numerados de nível 2 (7.1 a 7.4) com seus respectivos textos. Não há indicação de continuidade de conteúdo entre páginas. O cabeçalho está identificado como 'CXS 274-1969  5'.

A página contém um título principal em caixa alta sem numeração (APPENDIX- ADDITIONAL INFORMATION) com texto explicativo que continua para próxima página. Em seguida, há uma seção numerada nivel 1 '1. Method of manufacture' que funciona como agrupadora sem texto direto, com duas subseções numeradas (1.1 e 1.2) de nível 2 com texto associado, todos com títulos visíveis na página.
